steps.title: Find the y-intercept location
steps.content: The $y$-intercept occurs when $x = 0$. Substitute $x = 0$ into the function $g(x) = \frac{1}{8}(3)^{x}$.

steps.title: Evaluate the function at x = 0
steps.content:
$$g(0) = \frac{1}{8}(3)^{0}$$
Any nonzero number raised to the power 0 equals 1, so $(3)^{0} = 1$.
$$g(0) = \frac{1}{8} \cdot 1 = \frac{1}{8}$$
The $y$-intercept is the point $(0, \frac{1}{8})$. B is correct.
